message:
Add process_triage (pt) tool adapter for Bayesian agent health monitoring

- Add ToolPT constant to adapter.go and include in AllTools()
- Create pt.go with PTAdapter implementing full Adapter interface:
  - Detect() for binary detection on PATH
  - Version() for version parsing
  - Capabilities() for feature discovery
  - Health() for health checks
  - GetStatus() with caching for availability info
  - ClassifyProcess/ClassifyProcesses for PID classification
  - WatchSession for session-based monitoring
- Add comprehensive unit tests in pt_test.go (22 test cases)
- Register PTAdapter along with RCH, rano, caut adapters in init.go

Closes: bd-1h5xe

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1ghcp","title":"RCH: Implement rch binary detection and worker availability check","description":"Create utility function to detect RCH and check worker status.

## Requirements
1. Check if 'rch' binary exists on PATH
2. Verify rch version is compatible
3. Check if any workers are configured and available
4. Cache result with short TTL (workers may come/go)

## Implementation Location
Create new file: internal/integrations/rch/detect.go

```go
package rch

import (
    "encoding/json"
    "os/exec"
    "sync"
    "time"
)

type RCHStatus struct {
    Available    bool
    Version      string
    WorkerCount  int
    HealthyCount int
    LastCheck    time.Time
}

var (
    mu     sync.RWMutex
    status RCHStatus
    ttl    = 30 * time.Second
)

func GetStatus() RCHStatus {
    mu.RLock()
    if time.Since(status.LastCheck) < ttl {
        defer mu.RUnlock()
        return status
    }
    mu.RUnlock()
    
    return refresh()
}

func refresh() RCHStatus {
    mu.Lock()
    defer mu.Unlock()
    
    // Check binary
    // Run 'rch status --json'
    // Parse worker info
    status.LastCheck = time.Now()
    return status
}
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:07:32.155904396Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T10:07:34.350046341Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T10:07:34.349997629Z","close_reason":"Implementation complete: internal/tools/rch.go with RCHAdapter following the DCG adapter pattern.  {"id":"bd-1gkal","title":"Implement bulk prompt delivery","description":"Implement bulk prompt delivery.

## Functionality

Send assignment prompts to multiple panes efficiently.

## Default Prompt Template

```
Read AGENTS.md, register with Agent Mail. Work on: {bead_id} - {bead_title}.
Use br show {bead_id} for details. Mark in_progress when starting. Use ultrathink.
```

## Template Variables

- {bead_id}: The bead ID
- {bead_title}: Bead title/description
- {bead_type}: task, feature, bug, etc.
- {bead_deps}: Dependencies list
- {session}: Session name
- {pane}: Pane number

## Delivery Strategy

Option A: Sequential (safer)
- Send to pane 2, wait 1s
- Send to pane 3, wait 1s
- etc.

Option B: Parallel (faster)
- Send to all panes simultaneously
- May overwhelm if too many

Recommend: Sequential by default, --parallel flag for advanced users.

## Custom Templates

--prompt-template=<FILE> for custom prompts:
```
Custom marching orders here.
Bead: {bead_id}
```","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:31:28.015184270Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T23:31:28.043660276Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1gkal","depends_on_id":"bd-t9qp4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:31:28.043619780Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1h22","title":"Add --distribute flag to ntm send for auto-distributing work from bv --robot-triage","description":"## Overview
Add `--distribute` flag to `ntm send` that automatically distributes work from `bv --robot-triage` to agents.

## Implementation
This is a convenience shorthand that internally calls `ntm assign`. The full workflow is:
1. Get recommendations from `bv --robot-triage`
2. Match recommendations to available agents
3. Send individualized prompts to each agent via `ntm send --pane=N`

```bash
# Quick one-liner for distribution
ntm send myproject --distribute

# With options
ntm send myproject --distribute --limit=10 --cc-only
```

- [ ] --distribute flag parses correctly
- [ ] Calls bv --robot-triage internally
- [ ] Distributes N beads to N agents
- [ ] Logs which bead went to which agent
- [ ] Returns structured JSON in robot mode

## Dependencies
- Requires `ntm assign` command (bd-k3zq) as underlying implementation
- Requires `bv` integration (bd-55pm)

## Tests Required

 {"id":"bd-1h7g2","title":"Add --stagger flag for delayed agent launches","description":"Add --stagger SECONDS flag to control delay between agent launches.

## Purpose
Prevent overwhelming MCP Agent Mail when launching many agents at once.

## Syntax
```bash
ntm spawn myproject --cc=10 --cod=10 --stagger=30
```

## CRITICAL LESSONS LEARNED
1. Default stagger should be 30 seconds (not 20) - MCP Agent Mail gets overwhelmed
2. Wait 4 seconds after launching agent before sending marching orders
3. Send Enter TWICE after marching orders to ensure submission
4. Log confirmation that Enter was sent for debugging

## Behavior
- Default: 30 seconds (changed from 0 to protect MCP Agent Mail)
- When set: wait N seconds between each agent launch
- Progress indicator showing launch status with confirmation

## Implementation
- Add --stagger flag to spawn command (default: 30)
- Modify agent launch loop to include delay
- Show progress: [5/20] Launching agent... -> Sent message + Enter (x2)

## Code Pattern
```go
// After launching agent
time.Sleep(4 * time.Second)  // Wait for agent to initialize
sendKeys(pane, marchingOrders)
time.Sleep(1 * time.Second)
sendKeys(pane, "Enter")  // First Enter
time.Sleep(500 * time.Millisecond)
sendKeys(pane, "Enter")  // Second Enter - CRITICAL
log.Printf("  -> Sent message + Enter (x2)")
time.Sleep(staggerSeconds)
```","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T08:19:03.308328088Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T09:30:46.248833897Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-1hev","title":"Fix Agent Mail showing 'offline' when connected","description":"## Problem
The Agent Mail status always shows "offline" in the status bar even when the Agent Mail MCP server is available and connected.

## Screenshot Evidence
Status bar shows: "Mail: offline"

## Code Locations
- internal/tui/dashboard/dashboard.go - AgentMailUpdateMsg handling
- internal/agentmail/client.go - Agent Mail client connection
- internal/tui/dashboard/panels/ticker.go - Ticker panel rendering mail status

## Possible Causes
1. **Connection check failing** - Health check returns false even when server is up
2. **MCP server not detected** - Agent Mail MCP tools not being discovered
3. **Async timing** - Mail status check completing before dashboard ready
4. **Configuration issue** - Agent Mail URL/path not configured correctly
5. **Error swallowed** - Connection error not surfaced to UI

## Investigation Steps
1. Check Agent Mail MCP server status directly: \`curl localhost:PORT/health\`
2. Verify MCP tool discovery in Claude Code config
3. Add logging to agentmail.IsAvailable() and agentmail.IsConnected()
4. Check for errors in AgentMailUpdateMsg handling

## Diagnostic Code
\`\`\`go
func (c *Client) CheckStatus() AgentMailStatus {
    log.Debug("checking agent mail status",
        "endpoint", c.endpoint,
        "timeout", c.timeout)
    
    available := c.ping()
    log.Debug("agent mail ping result", "available", available)
    
    if !available {
        return AgentMailStatus{
            Available: false,
            Connected: false,
            Reason: "server not responding",
        }
    }
    
    connected := c.authenticate()
    log.Debug("agent mail auth result", "connected", connected)
    
    return AgentMailStatus{
        Available: available,
        Connected: connected,
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Fix Pattern
\`\`\`go
// Render mail status with more detail
func (t *TickerPanel) renderMailStatus() string {
    if !t.mailAvailable {
        return "Mail: unavailable"
    }
    if !t.mailConnected {
        return "Mail: disconnected"
    }
    if t.mailLocks > 0 {
        return fmt.Sprintf("Mail: %d locks", t.mailLocks)
    }
    return "Mail: ready"
}
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Shows "ready" when Agent Mail is available and connected
- [ ] Shows "disconnected" when server is up but auth fails
- [ ] Shows "unavailable" when server is down
- [ ] Shows lock count when file reservations exist
- [ ] Status updates on Agent Mail state changes

## Unit Tests
1. TestMailStatus_Available - server up shows "ready"
2. TestMailStatus_Unavailable - server down shows "unavailable"
3. TestMailStatus_Disconnected - auth failure shows "disconnected"
4. TestMailStatus_WithLocks - active locks shows count
5. TestMailStatus_Refresh - status updates on AgentMailUpdateMsg

## E2E Tests
1. Start dashboard with Agent Mail running - verify "ready"
2. Stop Agent Mail server - verify status changes to "unavailable"
3. Create file reservation - verify lock count appears
4. Invalid auth - verify "disconnected"

## Logging
- INFO: Log mail status changes
- DEBUG: Log connection check results
- DEBUG: Log lock count updates
- ERROR: Log connection failures with reason","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:09:20.163796969Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T09:17:57.561963735Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T09:17:57.561872172Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1hev","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:17:36.047093099Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Choose the safest, lowest‑dependency path to emit TOON.

## Options
1. **Go library**: embed TOON encoding directly in Go (preferred if stable library exists).
2. **CLI wrapper**: call `toon` binary (from `@toon-format/cli`) if installed; otherwise fallback to JSON or error.
3. **Minimal encoder**: implement TOON subset for NTM payloads (uniform arrays + primitives).

- TOON reference implementation is JS (`toon-format/toon`), CLI supports encode/decode and `--delimiter "\t"`.
- Spec lives in `toon-format/spec` (v3.0).

## Decision Criteria
- Reliability (no partial/invalid TOON)
- Minimal dependencies for Go build
- Graceful fallback behavior
- Token savings on real NTM payloads (see bd-rmnfk)

 {"id":"bd-2dovu","title":"EPIC: Robot Mode Ergonomics + Token Efficiency (TOON + JSON)","description":"## Goal
Make robot mode maximally ergonomic and token‑efficient for AI agents while preserving first‑class JSON support for tooling.

## Rationale
Robot mode is consumed primarily by AI agents (Codex/Claude/Gemini) where tokens and readability directly affect cost and accuracy. JSON is reliable for tooling but verbose; TOON can be far more compact for uniform arrays. We should support both and choose defaults based on evidence.

## Ground Truth
- `RobotResponse` already exists in `internal/robot/types.go` and is the canonical envelope.
- JSON output is currently pretty‑printed (stable and easy to diff) but not token‑efficient.

## Scope
- Evaluate TOON vs JSON for NTM robot payloads (token savings, clarity, failure modes).
- Add a robot output **format selector** (`json`, `toon`, `auto`) with config/env overrides.
- Standardize/validate the robot output envelope (reuse existing struct; no new schema).
- Provide token‑efficient profiles (terse vs full) **without** breaking parseability.
- Add pagination/limits where payloads are large.

## Non‑Goals
- No breaking removal of JSON.
- No dependency on a specific external CLI if not installed.

## Success Criteria
- Agents can request TOON output when beneficial.
- JSON remains first‑class and stable for scripts.
- Robot outputs are consistent and easier for LLMs to parse.
- Unit + E2E tests cover format selector, envelopes, and large payloads.","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-2dyqk","title":"caut: Implement background polling for usage data","description":"Create background goroutine that polls caut for usage data.

## Requirements

1. Poll caut at configured interval (default 60s)
2. Parse JSON output into internal structs
3. Update in-memory cache for dashboard
4. Trigger alerts when thresholds exceeded

## Implementation

```go
package caut

type UsagePoller struct {
    config   *config.CautConfig
    cache    *UsageCache
    interval time.Duration
    stopCh   chan struct{}
    alertCh  chan Alert
}

func (p *UsagePoller) Start() {
    go p.pollLoop()
}

func (p *UsagePoller) pollLoop() {
    ticker := time.NewTicker(p.interval)
    defer ticker.Stop()
    
    for {
        select {
        case <-ticker.C:
            usage, err := p.fetchUsage()
            if err != nil {
                // Log error, continue
                continue
            }
            p.cache.Update(usage)
            p.checkAlerts(usage)
        case <-p.stopCh:
            return
        }
    }
}

func (p *UsagePoller) fetchUsage() (*Usage, error) {
    cmd := exec.Command("caut", "status", "--json")
    // Parse output
}
```

## Code Location
- internal/integrations/caut/poller.go
- internal/integrations/caut/cache.go

- Mock caut output, verify parsing

 {"id":"bd-2pwzf","title":"Task: --robot-agent-health Command Implementation","description":"## Task: --robot-agent-health Command Implementation

### Why This Command Exists

While `--robot-is-working` answers "is this agent busy?", `--robot-agent-health` provides the FULL health picture by combining:

1. **Local state** - Parsed from pane output (via parser module)
2. **Provider usage** - Account-level limits from caut integration
3. **Health score** - Computed metric for decision-making

This enables sophisticated controller decisions like:
- "Agent is idle but account is at 90% - wait before sending more work"
- "Agent looks idle but we could not determine provider usage - proceed with caution"

### Command Specification

```bash
# Full health check (includes caut query)
ntm --robot-agent-health=myproject --panes=2

# Check multiple panes
ntm --robot-agent-health=myproject --panes=2,3,4

# Skip caut query (faster, local-only)
ntm --robot-agent-health=myproject --panes=2 --no-caut

# Include raw samples for debugging
ntm --robot-agent-health=myproject --panes=all --verbose
```

### Health Score Calculation (0-100)

```go
func calculateHealthScore(localState *agent.AgentState, providerUsage *caut.ProviderPayload) int {
    score := 100
    
    // Local state deductions
    if localState.IsRateLimited {
        score -= 50  // Critical - agent blocked
    }
    if localState.IsInError {
        score -= 40  // Serious - needs attention
    }
    if localState.IsContextLow && !localState.IsWorking {
        score -= 25  // Should restart soon
    }
    if localState.IsContextLow && localState.IsWorking {
        score -= 10  // Working but limited runway
    }
    if localState.Type == agent.AgentTypeUnknown {
        score -= 15  // Uncertainty penalty
    }
    
    // Provider usage deductions (if available)
    if providerUsage != nil {
        if pct := providerUsage.UsedPercent(); pct != nil {
            if *pct >= 95 {
                score -= 30  // Near cap
            } else if *pct >= 80 {
                score -= 15  // Approaching limit
            } else if *pct >= 60 {
                score -= 5   // Moderate usage
            }
        }
    }
    
    // Confidence adjustment
    if localState.Confidence < 0.5 {
        score -= 10  // Low confidence in assessment
    }
    
    // Floor at 0
    if score < 0 {
        score = 0
    }
    
    return score
}

func healthGrade(score int) string {
    switch {
    case score >= 90:
        return "A"
    case score >= 80:
        return "B"
    case score >= 70:
        return "C"
    case score >= 50:
        return "D"
    default:
        return "F"
    }
}
```

### Health Recommendations

| Recommendation | Trigger | Controller Action |
|----------------|---------|-------------------|
| `HEALTHY` | Score >= 70, no issues | Continue normally |
| `MONITOR` | Score 50-70, minor issues | Keep watching |
| `RESTART_RECOMMENDED` | Idle + low context | Restart when convenient |
| `RESTART_URGENT` | Error state or stuck | Restart immediately |
| `WAIT_FOR_RESET` | Rate limited | Wait for reset time |
| `SWITCH_ACCOUNT` | Provider at 90%+ | Consider caam account switch |

### Implementation Structure

```go
package robot

type AgentHealthResult struct {
    Session       string                       `json:"session"`
    Timestamp     string                       `json:"timestamp"`
    CautAvailable bool                         `json:"caut_available"`
    Panes         map[string]PaneHealthStatus  `json:"panes"`
    ProviderSummary map[string]ProviderStats   `json:"provider_summary"`
    FleetHealth   FleetHealthSummary           `json:"fleet_health"`
}

type PaneHealthStatus struct {
    AgentType            string              `json:"agent_type"`
    LocalState           LocalStateInfo      `json:"local_state"`
    ProviderUsage        *ProviderUsageInfo  `json:"provider_usage,omitempty"`
    HealthScore          int                 `json:"health_score"`
    HealthGrade          string              `json:"health_grade"`
    Issues               []string            `json:"issues"`
    Recommendation       string              `json:"recommendation"`
    RecommendationReason string              `json:"recommendation_reason"`
}

func AgentHealth(session string, panes []int, includeCaut bool) (*AgentHealthResult, error) {
    result := &AgentHealthResult{
        Session:       session,
        Timestamp:     time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
        Panes:         make(map[string]PaneHealthStatus),
        ProviderSummary: make(map[string]ProviderStats),
    }
    
    // Step 1: Get local state for all panes (--robot-is-working internally)
    isWorkingResult, err := IsWorking(session, panes, false)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to get working status: %w", err)
    }
    
    // Step 2: Query caut for provider usage (if enabled)
    var cautClient *caut.CachedClient
    if includeCaut {
        cautClient = caut.NewCachedClient(time.Minute * 5)
        result.CautAvailable = cautClient.IsAvailable()
    }
    
    // Step 3: Build health status for each pane
    for paneStr, workStatus := range isWorkingResult.Panes {
        healthStatus := PaneHealthStatus{
            AgentType: workStatus.AgentType,
            LocalState: LocalStateInfo{
                IsWorking:        workStatus.IsWorking,
                IsIdle:           workStatus.IsIdle,
                IsRateLimited:    workStatus.IsRateLimited,
                IsContextLow:     workStatus.IsContextLow,
                ContextRemaining: workStatus.ContextRemaining,
                Confidence:       workStatus.Confidence,
                Indicators:       workStatus.Indicators,
            },
        }
        
        // Query provider usage if caut available
        if cautClient != nil && result.CautAvailable {
            provider := caut.AgentTypeToProvider(workStatus.AgentType)
            if provider != "" {
                if usage, err := cautClient.GetProviderUsage(context.Background(), provider); err == nil {
                    healthStatus.ProviderUsage = convertProviderUsage(usage)
                    
                    // Track in provider summary
                    paneNum, _ := strconv.Atoi(paneStr)
                    updateProviderSummary(result.ProviderSummary, provider, usage, paneNum)
                }
            }
        }
        
        // Calculate health score and recommendation
        healthStatus.HealthScore = calculateHealthScore(&workStatus, healthStatus.ProviderUsage)
        healthStatus.HealthGrade = healthGrade(healthStatus.HealthScore)
        healthStatus.Issues = collectIssues(&workStatus, healthStatus.ProviderUsage)
        healthStatus.Recommendation, healthStatus.RecommendationReason = 
            deriveHealthRecommendation(&workStatus, healthStatus.ProviderUsage, healthStatus.HealthScore)
        
        result.Panes[paneStr] = healthStatus
    }
    
    // Step 4: Calculate fleet health summary
    result.FleetHealth = calculateFleetHealth(result.Panes)
    
    return result, nil
}
```

 {"id":"bd-6wc0a","title":"TOON Integration: Token-Optimized Object Notation for Robot Mode","description":"Integrate TOON (toon_rust) to reduce token usage in robot mode output by 40-60%.

## Project Location
/dp/toon_rust (Rust library + CLI)

## Problem Being Solved

Robot mode outputs JSON for every command (~60 commands). This JSON is:
- Read by AI agents (tokens cost money)
- Transmitted over network (bandwidth)
- Stored in logs (storage)

JSON is verbose with repeated keys, quotes, whitespace.

## What TOON Does

TOON is a token-efficient serialization format:
- 40-60% fewer tokens than equivalent JSON
- Designed specifically for LLM consumption
- Preserves type information
- Human-readable (unlike binary formats)
- Round-trip compatible with JSON

### Example
JSON:
```json
{"panes":[{"name":"agent-1","program":"claude-code","active":true}]}
```

TOON:
```
panes:[{name:agent-1,program:claude-code,active:T}]
```

## Integration Strategy for NTM

### Option A: Output Format Flag (Recommended)

## Options

### Option A: Subprocess (Simpler)
Call toon CLI as subprocess:
```go
cmd := exec.Command("toon", "encode")
cmd.Stdin = strings.NewReader(jsonData)
toonOutput, _ := cmd.Output()
```

Pros: Simple, no CGO, easy updates
Cons: Process overhead per call, latency

### Option B: CGO + Rust FFI (Faster)
Build toon_rust as C-compatible library, call from Go via CGO.

Pros: Near-zero overhead, native speed
Cons: CGO complexity, cross-platform builds, version coupling

### Option C: WASM (Middle Ground)
Compile toon_rust to WASM, run via Go WASM runtime.

Pros: Sandboxed, cross-platform, single binary
Cons: WASM overhead, runtime dependency

Start with **subprocess** for simplicity. If latency becomes issue, migrate to CGO.

Robot mode commands are not high-frequency (maybe 1-10/second), so subprocess overhead (~5ms) is acceptable.

+package tools
+
+import (
+	"bytes"
+	"context"
+	"encoding/json"
+	"fmt"
+	"log/slog"
+	"os/exec"
+	"strings"
+	"sync"
+	"time"
+)
+
+// PTAdapter provides integration with the process_triage tool.
+// process_triage uses Bayesian classification to identify useful, abandoned, and zombie processes.
+type PTAdapter struct {
+	*BaseAdapter
+}
+
+// NewPTAdapter creates a new process_triage adapter
+func NewPTAdapter() *PTAdapter {
+	return &PTAdapter{
+		BaseAdapter: NewBaseAdapter(ToolPT, "pt"),
+	}
+}
+
+// Detect checks if pt is installed
+func (a *PTAdapter) Detect() (string, bool) {
+	path, err := exec.LookPath(a.BinaryName())
+	if err != nil {
+		return "", false
+	}
+	return path, true
+}
+
+// Version returns the installed pt version
+func (a *PTAdapter) Version(ctx context.Context) (Version, error) {
+	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, a.Timeout())
+	defer cancel()
+
+	cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, a.BinaryName(), "--version")
+	var stdout bytes.Buffer
+	cmd.Stdout = &stdout
+
+	if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil {
+		return Version{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to get pt version: %w", err)
+	}
+
+	return parseVersion(stdout.String())
+}
+
+// Capabilities returns the list of pt capabilities
+func (a *PTAdapter) Capabilities(ctx context.Context) ([]Capability, error) {
+	caps := []Capability{}
+
+	path, installed := a.Detect()
+	if !installed {
+		return caps, nil
+	}
+
+	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, a.Timeout())
+	defer cancel()
+
+	cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, path, "--help")
+	var stdout bytes.Buffer
+	cmd.Stdout = &stdout
+	_ = cmd.Run() // Ignore error, just check output
+
+	output := stdout.String()
+
+	// Check for known capabilities
+	if strings.Contains(output, "--json") || strings.Contains(output, "classify") {
+		caps = append(caps, CapRobotMode)
+	}
+	if strings.Contains(output, "daemon") || strings.Contains(output, "watch") {
+		caps = append(caps, CapDaemonMode)
+	}
+
+	return caps, nil
+}
+
+// Health checks if pt is functioning correctly
+func (a *PTAdapter) Health(ctx context.Context) (*HealthStatus, error) {
+	start := time.Now()
+
+	path, installed := a.Detect()
+	if !installed {
+		return &HealthStatus{
+			Healthy:     false,
+			Message:     "pt not installed",
+			LastChecked: time.Now(),
+		}, nil
+	}
+
+	// Try to get version as a basic health check
+	_, err := a.Version(ctx)
+	latency := time.Since(start)
+
+	if err != nil {
+		return &HealthStatus{
+			Healthy:     false,
+			Message:     fmt.Sprintf("pt at %s not responding", path),
+			Error:       err.Error(),
+			LastChecked: time.Now(),
+			Latency:     latency,
+		}, nil
+	}
+
+	// Try a basic classify call to verify functionality
+	ctx2, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, 5*time.Second)
+	defer cancel()
+
+	// Try self-classify as a sanity check (classify our own process)
+	cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx2, path, "health", "--json")
+	var stdout, stderr bytes.Buffer
+	cmd.Stdout = &stdout
+	cmd.Stderr = &stderr
+
+	if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil {
+		// health command might not exist, try version-based health
+		return &HealthStatus{
+			Healthy:     true,
+			Message:     "pt is healthy (version check passed)",
+			LastChecked: time.Now(),
+			Latency:     latency,
+		}, nil
+	}
+
+	return &HealthStatus{
+		Healthy:     true,
+		Message:     "pt is healthy",
+		LastChecked: time.Now(),
+		Latency:     latency,
+	}, nil
+}
+
+// HasCapability checks if pt has a specific capability
+func (a *PTAdapter) HasCapability(ctx context.Context, cap Capability) bool {
+	caps, err := a.Capabilities(ctx)
+	if err != nil {
+		return false
+	}
+	for _, c := range caps {
+		if c == cap {
+			return true
+		}
+	}
+	return false
+}
+
+// Info returns complete pt tool information
+func (a *PTAdapter) Info(ctx context.Context) (*ToolInfo, error) {
+	return a.BaseAdapter.Info(ctx, a)
+}
+
+// pt-specific types and methods
+
+// PTStatus represents the availability and compatibility of pt on PATH.
+type PTStatus struct {
+	Available   bool      `json:"available"`
+	Compatible  bool      `json:"compatible"`
+	Version     Version   `json:"version,omitempty"`
+	Path        string    `json:"path,omitempty"`
+	LastChecked time.Time `json:"last_checked"`
+	Error       string    `json:"error,omitempty"`
+}
+
+// PTClassification represents a process classification result
+type PTClassification string
+
+const (
+	PTClassUseful    PTClassification = "useful"
+	PTClassAbandoned PTClassification = "abandoned"
+	PTClassZombie    PTClassification = "zombie"
+	PTClassUnknown   PTClassification = "unknown"
+)
+
+// PTProcessResult represents classification result for a single process
+type PTProcessResult struct {
+	PID            int              `json:"pid"`
+	Name           string           `json:"name,omitempty"`
+	Classification PTClassification `json:"classification"`
+	Confidence     float64          `json:"confidence"` // 0.0 to 1.0
+	Reason         string           `json:"reason,omitempty"`
+}
+
+var (
+	ptStatusCache  PTStatus
+	ptStatusExpiry time.Time
+	ptStatusMutex  sync.RWMutex
+	ptStatusTTL    = 5 * time.Minute
+	ptMinVersion   = Version{Major: 0, Minor: 1, Patch: 0}
+	ptLogger       = slog.Default().With("component", "tools.pt")
+)
+
+// GetStatus returns the current pt status with caching
+func (a *PTAdapter) GetStatus(ctx context.Context) (*PTStatus, error) {
+	ptStatusMutex.RLock()
+	if time.Now().Before(ptStatusExpiry) {
+		status := ptStatusCache
+		ptStatusMutex.RUnlock()
+		return &status, nil
+	}
+	ptStatusMutex.RUnlock()
+
+	status := a.fetchStatus(ctx)
+
+	ptStatusMutex.Lock()
+	ptStatusCache = *status
+	ptStatusExpiry = time.Now().Add(ptStatusTTL)
+	ptStatusMutex.Unlock()
+
+	return status, nil
+}
+
+// InvalidateStatusCache forces the next GetStatus call to re-check
+func (a *PTAdapter) InvalidateStatusCache() {
+	ptStatusMutex.Lock()
+	ptStatusExpiry = time.Time{}
+	ptStatusMutex.Unlock()
+}
+
+// IsAvailable returns true if pt is installed and compatible
+func (a *PTAdapter) IsAvailable(ctx context.Context) bool {
+	status, err := a.GetStatus(ctx)
+	if err != nil || status == nil {
+		return false
+	}
+	return status.Available && status.Compatible
+}
+
+func (a *PTAdapter) fetchStatus(ctx context.Context) *PTStatus {
+	status := &PTStatus{
+		LastChecked: time.Now(),
+	}
+
+	path, err := exec.LookPath(a.BinaryName())
+	if err != nil {
+		status.Error = err.Error()
+		ptLogger.Debug("pt binary not found", "error", err)
+		return status
+	}
+
+	status.Available = true
+	status.Path = path
+
+	version, err := a.Version(ctx)
+	if err != nil {
+		status.Error = err.Error()
+		ptLogger.Warn("pt version check failed", "path", path, "error", err)
+		return status
+	}
+
+	status.Version = version
+	if !ptCompatible(version) {
+		ptLogger.Warn("pt version incompatible", "path", path, "version", version.String(), "min_version", ptMinVersion.String())
+		return status
+	}
+
+	status.Compatible = true
+	return status
+}
+
+func ptCompatible(version Version) bool {
+	return version.AtLeast(ptMinVersion)
+}
+
+// ClassifyProcess classifies a single process by PID
+func (a *PTAdapter) ClassifyProcess(ctx context.Context, pid int) (*PTProcessResult, error) {
+	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, a.Timeout())
+	defer cancel()
+
+	cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, a.BinaryName(), "classify", "--pid", fmt.Sprintf("%d", pid), "--json")
+	var stdout, stderr bytes.Buffer
+	cmd.Stdout = &stdout
+	cmd.Stderr = &stderr
+
+	if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil {
+		if ctx.Err() == context.DeadlineExceeded {
+			return nil, ErrTimeout
+		}
+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("pt classify failed: %w: %s", err, stderr.String())
+	}
+
+	output := stdout.Bytes()
+	if !json.Valid(output) {
+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid JSON output from pt classify")
+	}
+
+	var result PTProcessResult
+	if err := json.Unmarshal(output, &result); err != nil {
+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse pt classify result: %w", err)
+	}
+
+	return &result, nil
+}
+
+// ClassifyProcesses classifies multiple processes
+func (a *PTAdapter) ClassifyProcesses(ctx context.Context, pids []int) ([]PTProcessResult, error) {
+	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, a.Timeout())
+	defer cancel()
+
+	// Build PID args
+	args := []string{"classify", "--json"}
+	for _, pid := range pids {
+		args = append(args, "--pid", fmt.Sprintf("%d", pid))
+	}
+
+	cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, a.BinaryName(), args...)
+	var stdout, stderr bytes.Buffer
+	cmd.Stdout = &stdout
+	cmd.Stderr = &stderr
+
+	if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil {
+		if ctx.Err() == context.DeadlineExceeded {
+			return nil, ErrTimeout
+		}
+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("pt classify failed: %w: %s", err, stderr.String())
+	}
+
+	output := stdout.Bytes()
+	if !json.Valid(output) {
+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid JSON output from pt classify")
+	}
+
+	var results []PTProcessResult
+	if err := json.Unmarshal(output, &results); err != nil {
+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse pt classify results: %w", err)
+	}
+
+	return results, nil
+}
+
+// WatchSession monitors agent processes in a session
+func (a *PTAdapter) WatchSession(ctx context.Context, sessionName string) ([]PTProcessResult, error) {
+	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, a.Timeout())
+	defer cancel()
+
+	cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, a.BinaryName(), "watch", "--session", sessionName, "--json", "--once")
+	var stdout, stderr bytes.Buffer
+	cmd.Stdout = &stdout
+	cmd.Stderr = &stderr
+
+	if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil {
+		if ctx.Err() == context.DeadlineExceeded {
+			return nil, ErrTimeout
+		}
+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("pt watch failed: %w: %s", err, stderr.String())
+	}
+
+	output := stdout.Bytes()
+	if !json.Valid(output) {
+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid JSON output from pt watch")
+	}
+
+	var results []PTProcessResult
+	if err := json.Unmarshal(output, &results); err != nil {
+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse pt watch results: %w", err)
+	}
+
+	return results, nil
+}
